stdout:
OBSERVATIONS:
- Image 1, category 2 (texture): the entire pane shows only a soft, low-frequency blotchy haze with no fine-grained ripple, roller-line, or hammered relief visible anywhere in the frame — even in the brightest, most saturated region there's no micro-texture, just smooth tonal blur.
- Image 1, category 6 (optics): the thin black thread/line running through the frame is high-contrast against a bright backlit field, yet its blurred edges show only neutral gray falloff — no purple/green chromatic fringing, which real lenses typically produce at such contrast edges under defocus.
- Image 1, category 6 (optics): the blur level is uniform across the entire depth of the frame (near edge, far background, foreground line) rather than showing a smooth handoff between in-focus and out-of-focus zones — inconsistent with a real lens's depth-of-field falloff.
- Image 1, category 3 (color depth): the warm-to-cool gradient (upper-left yellow to lower-right gray-green) is perfectly smooth and doesn't track any local structure — it reads as a flat gradient/haze laid over the surface rather than color changing with texture-driven thickness variation.
- Image 1, category 4 (edge): the right-side dark vertical band transitions with a uniform soft gradient and no distinguishable rim highlight, refraction line, or thickness-darkening detail.
- Image 2, category 1 (specularity): the lead came shows small, sharp glossy highlight speckles along its length (visible on the tail/body cames), consistent with a real point-source reflection on cylindrical metal.
- Image 2, category 2 (texture): the green and gray glass panels show directional mottled/streaky texture that varies pane-to-pane, consistent with real rolled cathedral glass.
- Image 2, category 6 (optics): faint sensor noise is visible in the darker cabinet/background areas, and there's slight motion blur on the hand at top — both realistic camera artifacts.

VERDICT: Image 1 synthetic -- its uniform blur with no fine glass texture, no chromatic aberration at a high-contrast edge, and a flat un-textured color gradient are inconsistent with a real macro photograph of glass.
